Notice Of Petition And Petition To Vacate Appraisal Award
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Dwight C. Donovan, of Fox Rothschild LLP, Tel. 415.364.5540, for plaintiffs;
Joel L. Halverson, of Severson & Werson APC, Tel. 415.398.3344, for defendants;
Adam Polakoff, of Severson & Werson APC, Tel. 415.398.3344, for defendants.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT NORMANDY APARTMENTS'S Petition To Vacate Appraisal Award is denied. Defendant timely filed and served the motion. Defendant properly served plaintiff's counsel rather than plaintiff directly because the instant petition/motion was part of this existing case and plaintiff was represented by counsel. CRPC 2-100(A) provides that an attorney should not directly communicate with a represented party. The court rejects defendant's argument that the award is somehow void because the preliminary injunction order was somehow void. If the injunction order was improper, defendant's remedy was to seek appellate relief. It is not for this court to reverse/second guess an order of another trial judge. In addition, any alleged impropriety in the injunction order is moot because the appraisal occurred. The written award/appraisal complies with the parties' partnership agreement because it sets forth the "opinions and the considerations on which of the opinions are based..." (Par. 20(b) of the Partnership Agreement). The award explains what data and factors were relied upon to render the valuation. The arbitrators did not exceed their powers. "Absent an express and unambiguous limitation in the contract or the submission to arbitration, an arbitrator has the authority to find the facts, interpret the contract, and award any relief rationally related to his or her factual findings and contractual interpretation." Gueyffier v. Ann Summers, Ltd. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 1179, 1182; see also Moshonov v. Walsh (2000) 22 Cal.4th 771, 773. Defendant fails to show that the award was the result of improper ex parte communications. First, the court strikes the Ricci declaration pursuant to Cal. Evid. Code sec. 703.5. Second, there is no showing that Mr. Clifford was having improper ex parte communications with plaintiff's representatives. Based on the instant record, it appears that the brokers (Mr. Pugh and Mr. Skele) were independent resources that were part of Mr. Clifford's "independent research and investigation." Defendant's failure to disclose argument has no merit because there was nothing to disclose. The court denies plaintiff's sanctions request. The court refrains from confirming the appraisal/award. A related ruling is currently on appeal and confirmation of the award may affect the appellate proceedings. See CCP 916(a). Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

ATTY. DAVID ERICKSEN OF SEVERSON & WERSON, TEL. NO. 677-5637 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT NORMANDY APARTMENTS' Motion To Vacate Preliminary Injunction is denied. Defendant waived the bond requirement. See Smith v. Adventist Health System/West (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 729. The opposition to the motion for preliminary injunction did not raise the bond issue. At the hearing, defendant only sought a bond with regard to the disbursements and the court ordered no injunctive relief as to the disbursements (Reporters Transcript pgs. 11 and 17). The injunction was properly ordered as the only means to compel the appraisal process. Defendant's motion is an improper motion for reconsideration and/or motion to dissolve that does not meet the requirements of CCP 1008 and CCP 533. Defendant does not highlight what material changes have occurred to warrant a change in the injunction order. Defendant fails to provide guidance regarding how paragraph 3 of the injunction should be amended to streamline the appraisal process. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
